sections.paragraph:
pr. subst. length of space, continuance of time (see the root, No. 2). Hence-

(1) [Chebar], pr.n. of a river in Mesopotamia, also called חָבֹור (which see), Greek and Latin Chaboras. Eze 1:3 3:15 , 23 Eze 3:23 10:15 , 22 Eze 10:22. This orthography of this name accords with the Syriac (ܟܒܰܪ, ܟܰܒܽܘܪ), while on the other hand חָבֹור (خابور) agrees with the Arabic. Although each form affords a suitable etymology (חָבֹור joining together, and כְּבָר length, a long, great river), yet I should regard the Aramean mode of spelling the name of a river in Mesopotamia, as the genuine and original.

(2) adv. already, long ago, formerly, now (längft). Ecc 1:10 3:15 4:2 9:6 , . (Syr. ܟܒܰܪ long ago, already).
